for the Field

Methodica's AgBot is a purpose-built autonomous ground vehicle engineered for precision agriculture — delivering intelligent spraying, mowing, fertilizing, and crop monitoring at scale.

Platform Overview

Intelligent Autonomy
for Modern Farming

The AgBot Autonomous Ground Vehicle combines advanced robotics, AI-driven perception, and precision agriculture systems to transform how farms operate — reducing labor costs, chemical waste, and environmental footprint.

  • 01
    Autonomous NavigationGPS/GNSS + LiDAR guided path planning with centimeter-level accuracy across complex terrain.
  • 02
    Multi-Task CapabilitySwitches between spraying, mowing, fertilizing, and scouting with modular attachments.
  • 03
    Zero-Emission PowertrainFully electric, 8+ hour runtime, reducing operational costs by up to 60%.
  • 04
    Real-Time Field IntelligenceNVIDIA edge AI processes crop health data, spray coverage, and anomaly detection live.
  • 05
    Remote Fleet ManagementCloud dashboard for task scheduling, telemetry, and OTA software updates.
AI-Enabled Precision Agriculture AgBot navigates row crops with <2 cm RTK GPS accuracy, optimizing inputs per plant.
Field Applications

Built for Every Task

From precision chemical application to autonomous crop monitoring, AgBot handles the full spectrum of agricultural operations across all major crop types and terrain.

Application 01

Precision Spraying

Variable-rate herbicide, pesticide, and fungicide application via 6-nozzle boom with 95%+ accuracy, reducing chemical usage by up to 40%.

Application 02

Autonomous Mowing

Robotic grass and cover-crop management with programmable lane patterns, blade height control, and real-time completion tracking on any terrain.

Application 03

Fertilizer Application

Zone-based variable-rate fertilization driven by NDVI heatmaps and soil sensor data, minimizing nutrient run-off and maximizing per-acre yield.

Application 04

Orchard Operations

Low-profile tracked variants navigate tight inter-row spacing in orchards and vineyards with LiDAR-guided tree avoidance and precision spraying.

Application 05

CEA Vertical Farm Operations

AgBot operates autonomously inside Controlled Environment Agriculture (CEA) vertical farms — navigating tight rows, monitoring plant health, and handling precision tasks without human intervention.

Application 06

Field Data Collection

Georeferenced GPS track logging with per-point soil EC, pH, temperature, and canopy data feeding directly into farm management systems.

Platform Variants

Three Platforms.
One Ecosystem.

One base platform. Multiple sector applications. Every AgBot configuration runs on the same autonomy stack — swap attachments to switch between Agri-Sprayer, Agri-Sense, and field data operations without retraining.

Agri-Sprayer Application

4WD Wheeled Platform

Purpose-built for field-scale chemical operations. The 4WD wheeled platform carries dual spray booms with variable-rate nozzle control. Covers large open fields, row crops, and hard-pack soil at up to 12 km/h.

Agri-Sense Application

Sleek Tracked Platform

Designed to "sense" and understand plant health across every row. Multispectral cameras, soil probes, and AI-vision modules mount on the tracked chassis for non-destructive, per-plant diagnostics at scale.

Heavy Duty · Rough Terrain

Industrial Tracked Platform

Heavy-duty chassis for high-payload operations on rough terrain and hillsides. Built for 200 L spray tanks and large-format implements.

Sensor Suite

Eyes, Ears & Intelligence
in Every Acre

AgBot's sensor array delivers centimeter-accurate positioning, 360° obstacle detection, live spray telemetry, and real-time environmental awareness across every field operation.

1

GNSS Localization & Navigation

Dual-antenna RTK-GNSS fusing GPS, GLONASS, and Galileo constellations for sub-2 cm positioning accuracy, reliable row-following, and autonomous headland turns.

Dual RTK-GNSS<2 cm AccuracyIMU FusionWheel Odometry
2

3D LiDAR Perception

360° rotating LiDAR builds a live point-cloud map enabling dynamic obstacle avoidance, crop-row detection, and real-time SLAM mapping across changing terrain.

360° LiDAR16–32 ChannelSLAM MappingObstacle Avoidance
3

Spray Feedback & Flow Metering

Inline flow meters, pressure transducers, and nozzle-status sensors monitor each spray channel in real time, detecting blockages and ensuring accurate variable-rate application.

Per-Nozzle Flow MetersPressure TransducersBlockage DetectionVariable Rate
4

Environmental & Crop Sensing

Anemometer, temperature, humidity, and multispectral cameras monitor wind for safe spray operations and generate live NDVI crop health indices per field zone.

Wind Speed/DirectionTemp & HumidityMultispectral CameraNDVI Mapping
Safety & Compliance

Safety by Design

Engineered to IEC 61508 SIL-2 with layered hardware and software safeguards at every level of the autonomy stack.

⚠

Emergency Stop

Physical E-stop + remote tablet + software watchdog trigger immediate motor cutoff and safe state.

👁

360° Detection

LiDAR + ultrasonic fusion detects humans, animals, and objects at up to 15 m, automatically slowing or stopping.

🔔

Geofence Lock

Pre-programmed field boundaries enforced at control layer — robot cannot operate outside defined zones.

✅

IEC 61508 SIL-2

Redundant sensing, dual-channel motor control, and independent watchdog MCUs meet functional safety requirements.

Deployment Journey

From Consultation to Harvest

A structured deployment process ensures your AgBot is calibrated, integrated, and supported from day one.

1

Field Assessment

Site survey, crop mapping, soil profiling, and connectivity audit to spec the right platform.

2

Configuration

Chassis selection, boom sizing, tank config, and software customization for your operation.

3

Commissioning

On-farm install, RTK base-station setup, fleet portal provisioning, and operator training.

4

Operations

Fully autonomous campaigns with live telemetry, spray logs, and agronomic data output.

5

Support

24/7 monitoring, OTA updates, agronomic consulting, and preventive maintenance programs.
